per_sample.expected: Fault 1017, customer had no dial tone and no broadband. The ONT was completely dead. Found the PSU output at zero volts. Swapped power supply, SKU PSU-12V-2A, all services back.
per_sample.got: Fault ten seventeen customer had no dial tone and no broadband, the ONT was completely dead, found the PSU output at zero volts, swapped power supply SKU PSU dash twelve v dash two a, all services back.

per_sample.expected: Ticket 1018, reported as a line fault but on attendance everything tested clean. Optical at -21 dBm, ONT stable. Turned out customer had unplugged their own router. No network fault.
per_sample.got: Ticket 118 reported as a line fault but on attendance everything tested clean. Optical at minus twenty one dBm. ONT stable. Turned out customer had unplugged their own router, no network fault.

per_sample.expected: Fault 1019, OLT reported a PON alarm. Found both a faulty SFP+ on the line card and a damaged patch cord in the ODF. Replaced SFP, SKU SFP-P-10G, and the patch lead, SKU PC-LC-APC-2M, alarm cleared.
per_sample.got: Fault ten nineteen, OLT reported an alarm, found both a faulty SFP+ on the line card and a damaged patch cord in the ODF, replaced SFP SKU SFP-P-TEN-G and the patch lead SKU PC-LC-APC-2M, alarm cleared.

per_sample.expected: Job 1020, booked as slow speeds. Actually found the ONT on old firmware causing rate capping. Upgraded firmware to 3.4, speeds back to full, no parts used.
per_sample.got: Job ten twenty booked as slow speeds, actually found the ONT on old firmware causing rate capping, upgraded firmware to 3.4, speeds back to full, no parts used.

per_sample.expected: Fault 1021, whole cabinet down reported as OLT failure, but root cause was a tripped breaker feeding the cabinet. Reset power and checked load, OLT and all ONTs recovered. Not a hardware fault.
per_sample.got: Fault one oh two one, whole cabinet down reported as OLT failure, but root cause was a tripped breaker feeding the cabinet, reset power and checked load, OLT and all ONTs recovered, not a hardware fault.

per_sample.expected: Ticket 1022, intermittent drops traced to a poorly seated line card in the OLT chassis. Reseated the card, no replacement needed, errors stopped.
per_sample.got: Ticket 1022, intermittent drops, traced to a poorly seated line card in the OLT chassis, reseated the card, no replacement needed, errors stopped

per_sample.expected: Fault 1023, no service to a new build. Found the drop had never been jumpered at the splitter. Jumpered the drop with a new patch, SKU PC-SC-APC-1M, service came up.
per_sample.got: Fault one thousand twenty three, no service to a new build, found the drop had never been jumpered at the splitter, jumpered the drop with a new patch SKU PC dash SC dash APC dash one M, service came up.

per_sample.expected: Job 1024, customer reported total loss. Engineer attended but it was a no-access, gated property, no answer. Card left, rebooked, no diagnosis possible.
per_sample.got: Job ten twenty four. Customer reported total loss. Engineer attended but there was no access. Gated property, no answer. Card left, rebooked, no diagnosis possible.

per_sample.expected: Fault 1025, high error rate on the PON. Found water ingress in a footway joint had corroded a splice. Re-spliced the affected fibres and fitted a new closure, SKU CLO-48, errors gone.
per_sample.got: Fault one oh two five. High error rate on the PON. Found water ingress in a footway joint had corroded a splice. Re-spliced the affected fibres and fitted a new closure SKU CLO-48. Errors gone.

per_sample.expected: Ticket 1026, ONU would not register, OMCI failing. Found the wrong service profile applied during commissioning. Reapplied the correct profile, ONU registered and traffic flowed.
per_sample.got: Ticket 1026, ONU would not register, OMC failing, found the wrong service profile applied during commissioning, reapplied the correct profile, ONU registered and traffic flowed

per_sample.expected: Fault 1027, marginal optical budget on a long drop. Measured -27 dBm. Replaced a suspect ONT, SKU ONT-5678, but no improvement. Real cause was an aged splitter, escalated for ODN works.
per_sample.got: Fault one thousand twenty seven, marginal optical budget on a long drop, measured minus twenty seven dBm, replaced a suspect ONT SKU ONT-5678 but no improvement, real cause was an aged splitter, escalated for ODN works

per_sample.expected: Job 1028, packet loss on the uplink. Found a failing SFP+ running hot on the OLT. Replaced SFP, SKU SFP-P-10G, CRC errors stopped and loss cleared.
per_sample.got: Job ten twenty eight, packet loss on the uplink, found a failing SFP+ running hot on the OLT, replaced SFP SKU SFP-P-TEN-G, CRC errors stopped and loss cleared

per_sample.expected: Fault 1029, customer reported slow WiFi. Tested the line and it was full speed at the ONT. Issue is the customer's own WiFi coverage. Advised on router placement, no fault on our network.
per_sample.got: Fault 1029, customer reported slow wifi, tested the line and it was full speed at the ONT, issue is the customer's own wifi coverage, advised on router placement, no fault on our network.

per_sample.expected: Ticket 1030, mass outage on a street. Digger struck the feeder duct and severed a 288-fibre cable. Emergency splice team restored with a new closure, SKU CLO-288, and spare fibre, service back to all homes.
per_sample.got: Ticket ten thirty, mass outage on a street, digger struck the feeder duct and severed a two eight eight fibre cable, emergency splice team restored with a new closure SKU CLO-288 and spare fibre, service back to all homes

per_sample.expected: Fault 1031, ONT rebooting randomly. Swapped the ONT, SKU ONT-5678, as a precaution but it kept happening. Found a flaky power socket in the property. Advised customer to use a different socket, stable after.
per_sample.got: Fault one thousand thirty one, ONT rebooting randomly. Swapped the ONT SKU ONT dash five six seven eight as a precaution but it kept happening. Found a flaky power socket in the property. Advised customer to use a different socket. Stable after.

per_sample.expected: Job 1032, no internet after a house move. Found the ONT had been provisioned on the wrong VLAN. Corrected VLAN to 500 and pushed the service profile, DHCP came up and internet worked.
per_sample.got: Job ten thirty two, no internet after a house move, found the ONT had been provisioned on the wrong VLAN, corrected VLAN to five hundred and pushed the service profile, DHCP came up and internet worked.

per_sample.got: Fault 1037, ONT showing dying gasp then no power, replaced both the ONT SKU ONT-5678 and its PSU SKU PSU-12V2A as the PSU had spiked and damaged the unit, service back.

per_sample.got: Ticket 1038 reported as slow speeds but actually intermittent total drops. Root cause was a failing drop cable with a hairline crack. Replaced drop cable SKU drop dash G657A2, stable.
